column1: Source: Avi Loeb, Medium — Nov 23, 2025  ·  Updated with new findings through Mar 2026  ·  Sorted chronologically by date first noted  ·  Loeb Scale: 3/10

column4: Retrograde orbit aligned within 5° of ecliptic plane
column6: Travels opposite to all planets yet nearly perfectly level with the flat plane they orbit in. A random interstellar object would be extremely unlikely to share this alignment.
column7: No natural explanation currently. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Arrival timing fine-tuned past Mars, Venus & Jupiter
column6: Path timed to pass within tens of millions of km from three planets while remaining hidden from Earth at perihelion. No natural explanation for this coincidence.
column7: No natural explanation currently. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column6: Hubble confirmed nucleus ~2.6 km diameter (Feb 2026) — at least 20,000× more massive than ʻOumuamua. Not enough rocky material in interstellar space to deliver an object this large by chance once per decade.
column7: No natural explanation currently. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Sunward jet (anti-tail) — physically real, not perspective
column6: Persistent jet pointing toward the Sun — opposite of what solar radiation causes. Confirmed by NASA's HiRISE at Mars closest approach. Pre-perihelion Hubble image shows jet ~10× longer than wide. Could be a particle shield against micrometeorite impacts at 60 km/s.
column7: No natural explanation currently. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Arrival direction aligns with the "Wow! Signal" within 9°
column6: 3I/ATLAS arrived from near Sagittarius — coinciding with the direction of the famous 1977 unexplained radio signal. Could be coincidence. No confirmed link.
column7: Could be statistical coincidence. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Extreme negative polarization — unprecedented for any comet
column6: Light reflected from 3I/ATLAS is polarized in a way never seen in any known comet. May relate to the unusual anti-tail or surface composition. No standard explanation.
column7: Could be statistical coincidence. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Nickel/iron ratio matches industrial alloys; anomalous cyanide ratio
column6: Gas plume has far more nickel than iron — matching industrially produced alloys — and a nickel-to-cyanide ratio orders of magnitude higher than any known comet including 2I/Borisov. May indicate artificial material composition.
column7: No natural explanation currently. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column6: Typical solar system comets are mostly water ice. Could reflect ices accumulated during interstellar travel rather than a primordial composition.
column7: Potentially explainable by unusual interstellar origin. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Brightened faster than any known comet; bluer than the Sun
column6: Rapid brightening near perihelion with anomalous blue color; faded faster after perihelion, showing unusual asymmetry. Possible signature of an engine activating. No confirmed natural explanation.
column7: Potentially explainable by unusual interstellar origin. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column6: Accelerated beyond gravity near perihelion, yet subsequent images show the object intact — ruling out mass-loss as the cause. Could be propulsion.
column7: Potentially explainable by unusual interstellar origin. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Jets require unreasonably large surface area for a natural comet
column6: The mass flux of the jets would need far more sunlight-absorbing area than a rock this size could provide. Jets could indicate technological thrusters.
column7: Potentially explainable by unusual interstellar origin. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Perijove matches Jupiter's Hill radius exactly
column6: The flyby distance to Jupiter on Mar 16, 2026 matches Jupiter's gravitational boundary almost exactly — enabled by 3I/ATLAS's own non-gravitational thrust near the Sun. Could indicate intentional deployment near Jupiter's Lagrange points.
column7: No natural explanation currently. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Jets maintain pointing direction despite object's rotation
column6: Jets stay precisely aimed across a million kilometers even as the nucleus rotates — inconsistent with passive sublimation from a spinning surface. Could indicate controlled thrusters.
column7: Potentially explainable by unusual interstellar origin. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Tri-carbon jet misaligned from all other molecular jets
column6: Keck telescope showed the tri-carbon (C3) emission jet points in a different direction than all other species (Ni, Fe, CN, C2) from the same nucleus. In a natural comet, different molecules should emit in the same direction. Currently unexplained.
column7: Potentially explainable by unusual interstellar origin. Listed in original Nov 2025 Loeb paper.

column4: Methane detected post-perihelion — a biosignature gas
column6: Webb detected methane (CH4) after perihelion — too volatile to survive a pass near the Sun in a natural comet, and a known biosignature. Pre-perihelion plume was dominated by nickel; post-perihelion it switched to methane. The chemistry switch is unexplained.
column7: Discovered after Nov 23, 2025 publication. Science still evolving.

column6: The world's most sensitive radio scan found no isotropic transmitters above 0.1 W. All candidate signals were Earth interference. Strongest counter-evidence to the alien technology hypothesis; most scientists cite this as supporting a natural comet interpretation.
column7: Counter-evidence. Breakthrough Listen radio silence is the strongest argument for a natural comet interpretation among post-publication findings.

column6: Hubble revealed three mini-jets around the nucleus, each separated by exactly 120°, alongside the main anti-tail. Seen in 40 post-perihelion images with periodic wobble. Random ice pockets would not naturally produce three-fold geometric symmetry. Could indicate symmetrically arranged thrusters.
column7: Discovered after Nov 23, 2025 publication. Science still evolving.

column4: Isotope ratios unlike any known Solar System body
column6: JWST found deuterium-to-hydrogen ratio >10× higher than in known comets. Carbon and nitrogen isotope ratios also exceed Solar System and nearby interstellar cloud values. Consistent with formation at below −240°C in a very ancient, metal-poor stellar environment — possibly billions of years old.
column7: Discovered after Nov 23, 2025 publication. Science still evolving.
